faq.label: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.title: Common Questions
faq.a1: NodoQuant is a quantitative analysis platform for traders. Upload your historical trades and the platform evaluates whether your strategy has real statistical edge.
faq.a2: You can upload exports from: MetaTrader 4, MetaTrader 5, Binance, and Generic CSV files.
faq.q3: What are the limits of the Free plan?
faq.a3: Free users can analyze trading datasets up to 500 trades. Saving and publishing strategies is available in the Pro plan.
faq.q4: How many trades do I need?
faq.a4: For reliable results we recommend at least 100 trades. With fewer than 30 trades the analysis may not be statistically reliable.
faq.a5: Yes. Your trading data is never shared. Only aggregated metrics are visible if you decide to share your report.
faq.q6: Do I need to connect my broker?
faq.a6: No. You only need to upload your trading history as CSV or platform export.
faq.q7: What if my file is not compatible?
faq.a7: If your file is not automatically detected, you can contact us and we will manually add it to our parsers.

metadata.title: Trading Strategy Analyzer — Discover Your Real Trading Edge | NodoQuant
metadata.description: Analyze any trading strategy instantly. Upload your trade history from MT4, MT5, or any CSV. Calculate Win Rate, Profit Factor, Expectancy, and Max Drawdown with our free strategy analyzer tool.
metadata.keywords: trading strategy analyzer, analyze trading strategy, forex strategy analyzer, crypto strategy analyzer, strategy performance analysis, backtest analyzer

hero.headline: Trading Strategy Analyzer — Discover Your Real Trading Edge
hero.subheadline: Stop guessing if your strategy works. Upload your trade history and get professional-grade quantitative analysis in under 60 seconds. Calculate your Win Rate, Profit Factor, and Expectancy for free.

steps.title: Export CSV Data
steps.desc: Export your trade history from your broker or platform as a CSV or Excel file. We support MT4, MT5, and most major standard formats.

steps.title: Automatic Parsing
steps.desc: Upload your file to NodoQuant. Our system automatically normalizes dates, symbols, and P&L into R-multiples for standardized analysis.

steps.title: Get Your Score
steps.desc: Instantly view your Strategy Score (0-100) along with detailed metrics on win rate, profit factor, and drawdowns.

features.title: Risk-Adjusted Performance
features.desc: We don't just look at total profit. We analyze your returns relative to the risk taken, giving you a true picture of your edge.

features.title: Win Rate vs Expectancy
features.desc: Understand the relationship between how often you win and your average win/loss size. This is the key to long-term profitability.

features.title: Drawdown Protection
features.desc: Analyze your worst periods to ensure your strategy is survivable during inevitable market shifts and high volatility.

features.title: Profit Factor Insights
features.desc: Calculate your gross profit over gross loss. A profit factor above 1.5 indicates a high-probability professional system.

features.title: R-Multiple Normalization
features.desc: We convert all trades to R-units so you can compare performance across different account sizes and risk levels fairly.

features.title: Actionable Insights
features.desc: Identify which symbols, market sessions, or trade types are dragging down your performance and which ones are your winners.

faqs.q: What is a Trading Strategy Analyzer?
faqs.a: A trading strategy analyzer is a software tool used by professional traders to evaluate the statistical performance of their trading history. It identifies whether profits are due to luck or a genuine edge.

faqs.q: How many trades are needed to evaluate a strategy?
faqs.a: Statistically, you need at least 30 trades for a basic signal, but 100+ trades are recommended for high-confidence results. More data helps filter out randomness (variance).

faqs.q: What is a good profit factor?
faqs.a: A profit factor above 1.0 means you are profitable. A profit factor above 1.5 is considered excellent, and above 2.0 is considered world-class.
Expectancy is the average amount you expect to win (or lose) per trade. It is calculated as (Win Rate × Avg Win) − (Loss Rate × Avg Loss). Positive expectancy is mandatory for success.

sampleSection.title: What is a Trading Strategy Analyzer?
sampleSection.p1: A **Trading Strategy Analyzer** is the most critical tool in a professional trader's arsenal. While most retail traders focus on finding the 'perfect entry', professional traders focus on **expectancy** and **risk-adjusted returns**.
sampleSection.p2: By using NodoQuant, you can determine if your recent winning streak was the result of a genuine **Trading Edge** or simply a streak of good luck within a random distribution. Conversely, an analyzer can show you if a losing streak is simply a normal statistical drawdown or a sign that your strategy no longer works in the current market environment.
sampleSection.h2_step: How to Analyze Your Trading Strategy Step-by-Step
sampleSection.p_step: Evaluating a strategy requires more than just looking at your account balance. Follow these steps to perform a professional audit:
steps: **Collect Data**: Export at least 50-100 trades from your broker.
**Normalize to R**: Convert your P&L into R-multiples (Risk units). This removes the bias of changing position sizes.
**Calcular Expectancy**: Usá la fórmula: `(Win Rate % * Avg Win) - (Loss Rate % * Avg Loss)`.
**Identify Variance**: Check your maximum drawdown to see if your emotional capital can handle the strategy's volatility.
sampleSection.h2_why: Why Strategy Analysis Matters for Your P&L
sampleSection.p_why: Without analysis, you are flying blind. Most traders abandon good strategies during a normal drawdown because they don't understand their strategy's **Max Drawdown** profile.
sampleSection.proTip: Professional trading is not about being right; it's about being profitable. A strategy analyzer shifts your focus from the 'outcome' of a single trade to the 'process' of a series of trades.
sampleSection.ready: Ready to see your real numbers?
sampleSection.h2_compare: Comparing Strategy Analyzers vs. Trading Journals
sampleSection.p_compare: While a trading journal is great for tracking your emotions and specific trade setups, a **Strategy Analyzer** like NodoQuant is focused on the quantitative output. Journals tell you *what* happened; Analyzers tell you *why* it's profitable (or not) from a mathematical standpoint.
sampleSection.finalLink: Upload your real trades and analyze your strategy →

faqs.a: Any exchange that exports trade history as CSV — including Binance, Bybit, OKX, Kraken, and Bitget. We also accept generic CSV formats.

faqs.q: Can I analyze spot and futures trades?
faqs.a: Yes. NodoQuant analyzes both spot and derivatives (futures/perps) trade histories from major exchanges.

faqs.q: How many crypto trades do I need for a reliable analysis?
faqs.a: At least 30 trades for basic results, 100+ for high-confidence strategy evaluation. We display a warning if your sample is too small.

faqs.q: Is my crypto portfolio data secure?
faqs.a: Your upload contains only historical trade records — no API keys, balances or personal information are required.

faqs.q: Can I test my Bitcoin or altcoin strategies?
faqs.a: Yes. Any cryptocurrency pair is supported — BTC, ETH, SOL, altcoins and derivatives. Results are currency-agnostic.

metadata.description: Analyze your Forex trading strategy with statistical precision. Upload MT4/MT5 trade history and calculate Win Rate, Profit Factor, Expectancy, and Max Drawdown. Free and safe.
metadata.keywords: forex strategy analyzer, EURUSD strategy analysis, forex expectancy calculator, MT4 strategy analysis, MT5 trading analyzer, forex trading edge

hero.headline: Forex Strategy Analyzer — Analyze Your Real Trading Edge
hero.subheadline: Upload your Metatrader (MT4/MT5) trade history and instantly calculate your Win Rate, Profit Factor, and Expectancy. Stop guessing and start trading with statistical confidence.

steps.title: Export MT4/MT5 History
steps.desc: Save your trade history as an HTML or CSV file from Metatrader 4 or 5. We also support standard broker formats through our smart parser.

steps.title: Smart Data Normalization
steps.desc: NodoQuant converts pips and currency gains into R-multiples. This allows you to evaluate your edge independently of position sizing or leverage changes.

steps.title: Professional Auditing
steps.desc: Get a full quantitative report showing your real win rate, profit factor, max drawdown, and expectancy per trade.

features.title: Global FX Support
features.desc: Analyze majors (EURUSD, GBPUSD), minors, and exotics. Filter performance by symbol to find which pairs you trade best.

features.title: Expectancy per Pip
features.desc: Understand how much you or your EA expects to earn per trade in relation to the risk taken. Positive expectancy is non-negotiable.

features.title: Drawdown Risk Analysis
features.desc: Forex markets are volatile. We calculate your worst-case peak-to-trough decline so you can manage your leverage safely.

features.title: Metatrader Native Support
features.desc: Our parser is optimized for MT4/MT5 reports. Simply drag and drop your history file directly into the analyzer.

features.title: Statistical Confidence
features.desc: Never trust a strategy with a small sample size. We flag datasets with under 50 trades to warn you about potential randomness.

features.title: Strategy Score
features.desc: A single number from 0 to 100 that tells you if your system is professional-grade, average, or high-risk.

faqs.q: How do I analyze an MT4/MT5 strategy?
faqs.a: Go to your 'Account History' tab in Metatrader, right-click and select 'Save as Detailed Report'. Then upload that HTML or CSV file to NodoQuant.

faqs.q: Is pip-based analysis better than R-multiple analysis?
faqs.a: No. Professional traders use R-multiples (risk-adjusted units) because pips don't account for stop-loss distance or position size.

faqs.q: Can I analyze Myfxbook or MetaStats data?
faqs.a: Yes, if you can export the data to CSV. NodoQuant is designed to be the next step beyond basic journals, providing deeper quantitative insights.

faqs.q: What is a good profit factor for Forex trading?
faqs.a: In the FX market, a profit factor between 1.2 and 1.8 is considered healthy. Anything consistently above 2.0 is exceptionally strong.
Yes. You can use our pair filtering to analyze only EURUSD trades and see if your edge differs between pairs.

content.h2_what: What is a Forex Strategy Analyzer?
content.p1: A **Forex Strategy Analyzer** is a quantitative tool designed specifically for the unique characteristics of the foreign exchange market. Unlike standard calculators, an FX analyzer must handle 24/5 liquidity and measure performance across currency pairs.
content.p2: For Metatrader users (MT4/MT5), NodoQuant provides an automated way to transform a 'Detailed Report' into a professional quantitative audit.
content.p_why: The #1 reason Forex traders lose money is not 'bad entries'. It is **poor risk management combined with a lack of statistical awareness**.
content.p_calc: By using our **Forex Expectancy Calculator**, you learn the limits of your strategy. If you know your system has a Max Drawdown of 15%, you won't panic when you lose 8% in a week.

card_mt.title: MT4/MT5 Compatibility
card_mt.desc: Simply export your history from the Metatrader terminal. We handle the rest. No manual data entry required.

card_pair.title: Pair Filtering
card_pair.desc: Analyze EURUSD breakout strategies separately from your Yen-pair carry trades. Find your niche.

content.p_master: The EURUSD is the most liquid pair in the world. A **Forex Analyzer** allows you to see if your edge remains consistent across sessions and symbols.
content.ready: Analyze your Forex EA or Manual History
content.readyFoot: Supports Metatrader, TradingView, and Standard Broker CSVs
content.h2_metrics: Key Quantitative Metrics for FX Trading
content.metric_stability: Recovery Factor: Net Profit / Max Drawdown. Tells you how fast you bounce back from losses.
content.p_leverage: Forex is a highly leveraged market. By analyzing your **Equity Curve**, you can visually see the impact of volatility and decide if your position sizing is responsible.
content.finalLink: Analyze your Forex strategy performance now →

metadata.title: Trading Edge Calculator — Know If Your Strategy Is Profitable | NodoQuant
metadata.description: Calculate your trading expectancy and profit factor with our free trading edge calculator. Learn the formula for success and how win rate impacts your returns.
metadata.keywords: trading edge calculator, expectancy calculator trading, profit factor calculator, trading expectancy formula, win rate calculator, trading edge meaning

hero.headline: Trading Edge Calculator — Know If Your Strategy Is Profitable
hero.subheadline: Use our interactive tool to calculate your statistical edge. Discover your expectancy, profit factor, and whether your risk-reward ratio is sustainable.

steps.title: Enter Your Stats
steps.desc: Use the interactive tool below to input your win rate, average win size, and average loss size to see your potential expectancy.

steps.title: Upload Real Data
steps.desc: While a calculator is great for estimates, NodoQuant allows you to upload actual trade history for a precise calculation based on real performance.

steps.title: Identify Your Edge
steps.desc: Our system calculates your Strategy Score, identifying if your system has a genuine edge or if you are simply experiencing variance.

features.title: Expectancy Calculator
features.desc: Your expected gain or loss per trade. Positive expectancy is the foundation of trading.

features.title: Profit Factor
features.desc: The ratio of gross profits to gross losses. Anything above 1.5 is considered high-tier.

features.title: Win Rate Analysis
features.desc: See how your winning percentage interacts with your risk-reward ratio. You don't need a high win rate to be profitable.

features.title: Drawdown Awareness
features.desc: Estimate your potential capital risk. Understanding drawdown is key to staying in the game during losing streaks.

features.title: Edge Score (0–100)
features.desc: Get a single score that measures the overall robustness and reliability of your trading edge.

features.title: R-Multiple Logic
features.desc: Learn to think in terms of risk units (R) instead of dollars, the secret language of professional multi-market traders.

faqs.a: A trading edge is a statistical advantage that makes your strategy profitable over a large enough sample of trades. It means that the probability of success is higher than the probability of failure.

faqs.q: What is the formula for trading expectancy?
faqs.a: Expectancy = (Win Rate × Average Win) - (Loss Rate × Average Loss). If the result is positive, your strategy has an edge.

faqs.q: What is a good profit factor?
faqs.a: A profit factor above 1.0 means you're profitable. Above 1.5 is great, and above 2.0 is considered professional grade.

faqs.q: How many trades do I need for a reliable calculation?
faqs.a: Industry experts recommend at least 100 trades to have high statistical confidence. Smaller samples are often skewed by luck.

faqs.q: Can I use this calculator for Forex and Crypto?
faqs.a: Yes. Trading expectancy and edge calculations are market-agnostic. They work for Forex, stocks, crypto, and futures equally.

content.p_interactive: Adjust the sliders and values to see how different metrics impact your strategy's expectancy.
content.p1: In the world of professional speculation, a **Trading Edge** is much more than a 'setup' or a signal. It is a **statistical advantage** that allows you to profit over an extended series of trades.
content.p2: Having an edge means your **Expectancy** is positive. This implies that for every dollar you put into the market, you expect to receive that dollar back plus a surplus.
content.h3_how: How to Calculate Your Trading Expectancy
content.p_how: Expectancy is the holy grail of trading math. It combines your **Win Rate** and your **Average Risk-to-Reward Ratio**.
content.p_example: Example: If you win 40% of the time, and your average win is $300 while your average loss is $100:
content.p_result: This means you earn an average of **$60 per trade** across a large enough sample size.
content.h3_not_enough: Why the Calculator is Not Enough
content.p_calc_limit: A calculator is perfect for **planning** and **forecasting**. However, the real market includes slippage, commissions, and emotional errors.
content.p_reality: This is why professional traders use NodoQuant to analyze their **actual trade history**. By uploading your real trades, you move from 'theory' to 'reality'.

card_csv.title: Do you have 30+ trades in a CSV file?
card_csv.desc: Stop guessing your stats. Get your real Strategy Score and see your equity curve now.

content.h2_winrate: The Relationship Between Win Rate and R:R
content.p_winrate: One of the biggest mistakes novice traders make is obsessing over a high win rate. In reality, a strategy with a 25% win rate can be significantly more profitable if the ratio is high enough.
content.h3_metrics: Key Metrics for Your Edge Report
content.finalLink: Calculate your edge with real trade data →

ranking.top5: This strategy performs better than 95% of tested strategies.
ranking.top20: This strategy performs better than 80% of tested strategies.
ranking.top40: This strategy performs better than 60% of tested strategies.
ranking.bottom50: This strategy performs better than 30% of tested strategies.
ranking.bottom20: This strategy is in the bottom 20% of tested strategies.

narrative.elite: Your strategy shows exceptional statistical robustness and risk control.
narrative.strong: Your strategy shows signs of a statistical edge based on historical trades.
narrative.promising: Promising performance metrics. Some optimizations could lead to elite results.
narrative.unstable: Profitable but vulnerable. Review drawdown periods to identify potential risks.
narrative.none: Current data does not show a reliable statistical edge.
narrative.gainR: Your strategy earns +{val}R per trade on average — that's real statistical edge.
narrative.breakEvenR: Your strategy breaks even with 0.00R per trade. The edge needs improvement.
narrative.lossR: Your strategy loses {val}R per trade on average — review entrance/exit rules.
narrative.highPf: Profit Factor {val} — your strategy returns {val} units for every 1 unit lost. A solid edge.
narrative.marginalPf: Profit Factor {val} is profitable but marginal. Small improvements in R:R or entry timing could significantly increase returns.
narrative.lowPf: Profit Factor below 1.0 means losses exceed gains. Review entry timing and stop loss placement before live trading.

edgeQuality.title: Edge Quality
edgeQuality.tipSolid: Run a Monte Carlo analysis to validate if this edge holds under simulated variations.
edgeQuality.tipMarginal: Focus on improving Risk:Reward. Even a small increase can push PF above 1.5.
edgeQuality.tipNone: Review your last 20 losing trades to identify repeated entry errors.

riskProfile.title: Risk Profile
riskProfile.tipControlled: Check if this drawdown remains stable across different market sessions.
riskProfile.tipModerate: Add a daily loss limit to prevent drawdown streaks that erode the account.
riskProfile.tipHigh: Reduce your position sizes by 50% until Max DD stabilizes below 20%.

sample.title: Sample Reliability
sample.tipRobust: Monitor if metrics drift as you add the next 50 trades to ensure stability.
sample.tipModerate: Continue trading the same plan without changes until reaching 100 trades.
sample.tipLow: Focus on execution consistency rather than results until the sample exceeds 50.

location.title: Edge Location
location.tipOptimal: Consider trading ONLY during the {session} session on your best days.
location.tipInsufficient: Ensure your CSV includes 'symbol' and 'datetime' columns for deep analysis.
